title: Counting the cost of F1's controversial new engine formula
summary: In 1954, the world championship adopted a 2.5-litre engine formula whose cack-handed introduction had led directly to grands prix having to be opened to Formula 2 cars only for the preceding two seasons. F1 has changed dramatically over the winter – and that couldn't simply not create some controversy.
